# Architectural Drawing Description

This is a historical architectural blueprint on aged parchment showing detailed construction drawings for a Victorian-era building complex. The main illustration in the lower portion displays a longitudinal section view labeled "Longitudinal Section" showing the full side profile of an ornate structure with multiple interconnected components. The building features a prominent tall tower with a pointed spire on the left side, several peaked roof sections of varying heights, multiple windows and doorways, decorative cornices, and an elaborate foundation with supporting pillars. The structure spans across the entire width of the drawing with detailed measurements and construction notations marked throughout. In the upper right, a transverse section view labeled "Transverse Section" provides a cross-sectional perspective showing the interior height relationships and structural framework, including roof trusses and internal support systems. Small handwritten annotations in period script appear in the upper left and scattered throughout the drawing, indicating architectural notes and specifications. The document displays the characteristic tan coloring of aged paper with various brown ink lines used for the technical drawings. A color reference scale appears along the right edge of the document. The overall presentation represents a comprehensive architectural record typical of 19th-century construction documentation.

# Architectural Floor Plan - Second Floor

This is a historical architectural blueprint drawn on aged, cream-colored paper with visible stains and wear marks. At the top is a color reference bar and measurement scale. The document shows a detailed second floor plan with brown ink lines indicating room layouts, walls, and architectural features. Multiple rooms are labeled as "Bed Room" and "Sitting Room," with precise measurements noted in feet and inches throughout. # Architectural Floor Plan Description

This is a detailed architectural drawing on aged, cream-colored paper showing the first floor layout of a residential building. At the top of the document is a measurement scale and color reference bar. The plan is drawn in brown ink with handwritten annotations labeling various rooms and dimensions.

The layout is symmetrical and organized vertically, featuring multiple levels of rooms arranged around central circulation spaces. Key rooms visible include a large central "Kitchen" (approximately 18'×15'), flanked by two substantial rooms labeled "Old Ladies Dining Room" (20'×30') on the left and "Children's Dining Room" (20'×30') on the right. 

Above these are smaller spaces including a "Scullery" (labeled with dimensions), pantries, parlors, and what appears to be sitting areas. The upper section contains bedrooms and private chambers connected by corridors and staircases.

The lower portion of the plan shows additional rooms including a "Hall," multiple "Bed Rooms" of varying sizes (ranging approximately 12'×15' to larger dimensions), a "Billiards Room," "Sitting Room," and service areas like a "Servants' Room" and "Servants' Stairs."

The drawing indicates "SCALE: Eight Feet To One Inch" at the bottom left. In the lower right corner, the architect attribution reads "E.M. CROSSALL & SON, ARCHTS. 930 PINE ST. S.F." The document is labeled "FIRST FLOOR" at the bottom center.
